name: The Hero's Journey
description: A protagonist departs from the ordinary world, undergoes trials and transformation in a special world, and returns changed. The structure emphasizes initiation, crisis, and reintegration.

nodes.label: Ordinary World
nodes.definition: The protagonist's familiar, stable environment before any call to change. Establishes what is normal and what the character values or lacks.
exit_conditions: A disruption or summons arrives that cannot be ignored
typical_variants: Idyllic peace that masks inner dissatisfaction
failure_modes: World is so bland the audience has no reason to care about what the character might lose
Ordinary world is never clearly established, so departure has no weight
signals_in_text: Contrast between character's life and the wider world

nodes.label: Call to Adventure
nodes.definition: An external event, message, or encounter that presents the protagonist with a problem or opportunity that the ordinary world cannot resolve.
exit_conditions: The protagonist acknowledges the call exists (acceptance or refusal)
typical_variants: Discovery of a mystery or injustice
failure_modes: Call is too vague to create urgency
Call is so overwhelming it removes choice entirely

nodes.label: Refusal of the Call
nodes.definition: The protagonist hesitates or declines the call, revealing fear, obligation, or attachment to the ordinary world.
exit_conditions: An escalation or new event overrides the refusal
typical_variants: Disbelief that the call is real
failure_modes: Refusal lasts so long it stalls momentum
Refusal has no credible basis

nodes.label: Meeting the Mentor
nodes.definition: The protagonist encounters a figure who provides guidance, tools, or confidence needed to accept the journey.
entry_conditions: Protagonist lacks something needed to proceed
exit_conditions: Protagonist has received what the mentor can give at this stage
typical_variants: A text, artifact, or inherited knowledge
failure_modes: Mentor solves problems for the hero, removing agency
Mentor's gift has no cost or limit
signals_in_text: Gift of a weapon or talisman
Cryptic advice that pays off later

nodes.label: Crossing the Threshold
nodes.definition: The protagonist leaves the ordinary world and enters the special world where the adventure's rules apply. This is the first irreversible step.
entry_conditions: Protagonist has accepted the call (possibly after refusal)
exit_conditions: The protagonist is fully in the special world with no easy return
typical_variants: Social departure (breaking a rule, defying authority)
failure_modes: Crossing is trivial with no sense of risk
Character can easily return, undermining irreversibility

nodes.label: Road of Trials
nodes.definition: A series of tests, enemies, and allies encountered in the special world that build the protagonist's competence and reveal the world's rules.
entry_conditions: Protagonist is in the special world
Central conflict is not yet at peak
exit_conditions: Protagonist has gained enough experience or allies for the next major challenge
failure_modes: Trials are too easy and cost nothing
Trials are disconnected from the central conflict
No progression in difficulty or learning

nodes.label: Approach to the Inmost Cave
nodes.definition: The protagonist prepares for the central ordeal, entering the most dangerous region of the special world.
entry_conditions: The source of the central threat is identified
exit_conditions: The protagonist stands at the threshold of the supreme ordeal
failure_modes: No sense of escalating danger
Approach is skipped, making the ordeal feel sudden

nodes.label: The Ordeal
nodes.definition: The protagonist faces the greatest challenge — a confrontation with death, the shadow, or the central antagonistic force. This is the story's structural climax of the inner journey.
entry_conditions: Protagonist has reached the inmost cave
All prior preparation is now tested
failure_modes: Ordeal is won too easily
Ordeal has no personal cost
Outcome feels arbitrary rather than earned

nodes.label: Reward (Seizing the Sword)
nodes.definition: Having survived the ordeal, the protagonist claims the prize — knowledge, power, reconciliation, or an object of great value.
exit_conditions: The protagonist possesses or embodies the reward
failure_modes: Reward feels unearned
Reward is never used or tested
Reward is handed over without effort

nodes.label: The Road Back
nodes.definition: The protagonist begins the return to the ordinary world but faces new dangers or temptations that test whether the transformation will hold.
entry_conditions: The special world is not yet left behind
exit_conditions: The protagonist commits to completing the return
typical_variants: Chase or pursuit by remaining antagonists
Temptation to stay in the special world
Moral test of whether to use the reward selfishly
failure_modes: Return is uneventful
No tension about whether the character has truly changed

nodes.label: Resurrection
nodes.definition: A final, climactic test where the protagonist must apply everything learned. This is the external climax — the last purification before return.
entry_conditions: The road back has presented a final threat
The stakes encompass more than just the protagonist
exit_conditions: The protagonist definitively resolves the central conflict
failure_modes: Feels redundant after the ordeal
Resolved by external force rather than the hero's growth

nodes.label: Return with the Elixir
nodes.definition: The protagonist returns to the ordinary world carrying the reward or wisdom gained, which benefits the community or transforms the character's place in it.
entry_conditions: The character is ready to reintegrate
failure_modes: Return is glossed over
Elixir has no visible impact on the ordinary world
Character seems unchanged despite the journey

edges.label: Call Received
edges.effects_on_stakes: Introduces the central problem; stakes shift from personal comfort to an external demand
edges.effects_on_character: Forces the protagonist to acknowledge a world beyond the familiar
edges.common_alternatives: Call arrives through loss rather than opportunity
edges.anti_patterns: Call is so gentle it creates no urgency

edges.label: Hero Refuses
edges.preconditions: Call has been received; character has reason to resist
edges.effects_on_stakes: Delays engagement, often causing the threat to worsen
edges.anti_patterns: Refusal has no consequences; nothing changes while the hero hesitates

edges.label: Refusal Overridden
edges.preconditions: Refusal is in place; an escalation occurs
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes increase because delay has worsened the problem
edges.effects_on_character: Character is compelled past their resistance
edges.common_alternatives: External force removes the option to refuse (e.g., home is destroyed)
edges.anti_patterns: Override is trivial; no cost to having refused

edges.label: Seeks Guidance
edges.preconditions: Call is accepted (or refusal bypassed); protagonist recognizes a gap in ability
edges.effects_on_stakes: Protagonist gains resources but also new obligations
edges.effects_on_character: Shifts from isolation to connection; accepts help
edges.common_alternatives: Mentor is already present and reveals role; mentor is an object or text
edges.anti_patterns: Mentor appears with no reason; mentor gives everything needed with no limit

edges.label: Crosses into Special World
edges.preconditions: Mentor's aid is received; protagonist has what they need to begin
edges.effects_on_stakes: Irreversibility established; the ordinary world is behind
edges.effects_on_character: Identity begins shifting from ordinary person to quester
edges.common_alternatives: Threshold is crossed under duress rather than by choice
edges.anti_patterns: Crossing is trivially reversible; no sense of leaving something behind

edges.label: Enters Trials
edges.preconditions: Protagonist is in the special world; challenges present themselves
edges.effects_on_stakes: Progressive escalation of danger and difficulty
edges.effects_on_character: Skills, alliances, and self-knowledge develop through testing
edges.common_alternatives: First trial is immediately upon crossing; trials emerge gradually
edges.anti_patterns: Trials are unconnected to the central conflict

edges.label: Approaches the Core
edges.preconditions: Sufficient trials are passed; the source of the central threat is located
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist must face what they've been building toward
edges.common_alternatives: Approach is forced by the antagonist rather than chosen
edges.anti_patterns: No escalation between trials and the ordeal

edges.label: Faces the Ordeal
edges.preconditions: Protagonist is at the inmost cave; all preparation is complete
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum — life, identity, or the mission is at risk
edges.effects_on_character: Deepest fears and flaws are confronted directly
edges.common_alternatives: Ordeal is sprung as a trap rather than approached deliberately
edges.anti_patterns: Ordeal is easily overcome; no sense of genuine peril

edges.label: Claims the Reward
edges.preconditions: The ordeal is survived; the cost has been paid
edges.effects_on_stakes: Immediate pressure drops; new responsibility arises from possessing the reward
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist is fundamentally altered by the ordeal
edges.common_alternatives: Reward is knowledge rather than object; reward is bittersweet
edges.anti_patterns: Reward appears without the ordeal being resolved

edges.label: Begins Return
edges.preconditions: Reward is in hand; the special world is no longer the destination
edges.effects_on_stakes: New threats emerge from possessing the reward or from the antagonist's remnants
edges.effects_on_character: Tests whether the transformation is durable under pressure
edges.common_alternatives: Return is voluntary; return is forced by collapse of the special world
edges.anti_patterns: Return is uneventful; no remaining opposition

edges.label: Final Test
edges.preconditions: A final threat materializes on the road back
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes expand beyond the protagonist to encompass the community
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist must prove transformation is real under ultimate pressure
edges.common_alternatives: Resurrection is internal (moral choice) rather than external (battle)
edges.anti_patterns: Final test is redundant with the ordeal; resolved by luck

edges.label: Returns Transformed
edges.preconditions: The resurrection test is passed; the conflict is resolved
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes dissolve; a new normal is established
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist is reintegrated into the ordinary world as a changed person
edges.common_alternatives: Return is to a new community rather than the old one; the elixir transforms others
edges.anti_patterns: Return is glossed over; no visible impact of the journey on the world
